description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages, build and deploy, and no global image. In the build stage add a job named build that runs `mkdir .public`, `cp -r * .public`, and `mv .public public`, then publishes the resulting public/ directory as an artifact. In the deploy stage add a job named "deploy to production" that uses the python:latest Docker image, runs only on the master branch, sets the variable S3_BUCKET_NAME to improbible.com, and runs `pip install awscli`, `aws s3 rm s3://$S3_BUCKET_NAME --recursive`, `aws s3 cp ./public/ s3://$S3_BUCKET_NAME/ --recursive --exclude ".git/*" --include "*" --acl public-read`, and `aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id $CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID --paths "/*"`. The deploy job targets the production environment and updates the S3 bucket and CloudFront distribution.
